subtopic: Energy Transition and Climate Risk Indices
title: State of the Cryosphere Report 2025
focus: Publisher and sector coverage of the report.
rationale: A brief but distinct report requiring knowledge of its publisher and sector focus.
sectionExcerpt: State of the Cryosphere Report 2025 - Released by: International Cryosphere Climate Initiative - Sector Wise Coverage: Power followed by industry sector have highest coverage.
evidenceBullets: Released by the International Cryosphere Climate Initiative.
Power and industry sectors have the highest coverage in the report.

subtopic: Energy Transition and Climate Risk Indices
title: Energy Transition Index (ETI) 2025
focus: Publisher, global rankings, India's rank, and assessment parameters.
rationale: Highly examinable index by WEF with specific parameters and ranking shifts for India.
sectionExcerpt: Energy Transition Index (ETI), 2025 - Released by: World Economic Forum (WEF) - Ranks countries on their progress towards energy transition from fossil fuels to clean energy. - Sweden ranked first followed by Finland, Denmark and Norway. - India's rank dropped from rank 63 in 2024 to rank 71 in 2025. - Takes into account two main aspects: System Performance (energy security, equity & sustainability) and Transition Readiness (regulation, infrastructure, investment etc.).
evidenceBullets: Released by the World Economic Forum (WEF).
Sweden ranked first in the 2025 index.
India's rank dropped to 71 in 2025 from 63 in 2024.
Assesses System Performance (energy security, equity, sustainability) and Transition Readiness (regulation, infrastructure, investment).

subtopic: Energy Transition and Climate Risk Indices
title: Climate Risk Index (CRI) 2026
focus: Publisher, ranking basis, and India's historical and current rankings.
rationale: Important index by Germanwatch focusing on the human and economic toll of extreme weather.
sectionExcerpt: Climate Risk Index (CRI) 2026 - Published by: Germanwatch - Ranks countries based on human and economic toll of extreme weather events. - Introduced in 2006 as an annual global climate impact index. - India ranked 15th in CRI 2024 and 9th in CRI for 1995-2024.
evidenceBullets: Ranks countries based on the human and economic toll of extreme weather events.
India ranked 15th in CRI 2024 and 9th for the period 1995-2024.

subtopic: Energy Transition and Climate Risk Indices
title: Climate Change Performance Index (CCPI) 2026
focus: Publishers, top rankings, India's rank, and the four measurement categories.
rationale: A major climate index with multiple publishers, specific measurement categories, and notable ranking details such as an empty top 3.
sectionExcerpt: Climate Change Performance Index (CCPI) 2026 - Published by: Germanwatch, New Climate Institute & Climate Action Network (CAN). - Compares climate performance of 63 countries and the EU, which together account for over 90% of global GHG emissions. - None of the countries are in the top 3. Denmark is 4th, followed by UK & Morocco. - India ranks 23rd, going from a high performer to a medium one in this year's CCPI. - Measures performance in 4 categories: GHG Emissions, Renewable Energy, Energy Use and Climate Policy.
evidenceBullets: Published by Germanwatch, New Climate Institute, and Climate Action Network (CAN).
Evaluates 63 countries and the EU, covering over 90% of global GHG emissions.
No country achieved the top 3 ranks; Denmark is ranked highest at 4th.
India ranks 23rd, dropping from a high performer to a medium performer.
Measures performance across 4 categories: GHG Emissions, Renewable Energy, Energy Use, and Climate Policy.
